topic.what: Multi-stage LLM agent framework for detecting and confirming taint-style vulnerabilities (command injection, code injection, path traversal, prototype pollution) in Node.js packages
topic.how: ReAct-based agent pipeline using LLM reasoning without dedicated static/dynamic analysis engines for path derivation
topic.why_matters: npm ecosystem contains millions of packages critical to software supply chains; traditional program analysis struggles with JavaScript dynamic features and large dependencies

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of benchmark vulnerabilities confirmed with valid exploits
characteristics.evidence: Table showing confirmation rates on VulcaN and SecBench.js datasets
characteristics.context: Compared to FAST, NodeMedic-FINE, and Explode.js on public benchmarks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of previously undocumented vulnerabilities found in recently released packages
characteristics.evidence: Evaluation on 260 recently released npm packages (December 2025)
characteristics.context: All 36 vulnerabilities reported to maintainers with 3 acknowledgments received

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to filter out false positives early in pipeline
characteristics.evidence: Multi-stage pipeline with Judge stage filtering infeasible hypotheses
characteristics.context: Achieved through exploitability filtering stage and execution oracles

characteristics.evidence: Execution oracles validate concrete side effects rather than model self-reports
characteristics.context: Oracle-guided exploit confirmation provides automated success predicates

characteristics.vs_baseline: command injection, code injection, path traversal, prototype pollution
characteristics.context: Same vulnerability types as prior program-analysis tools (FAST, NodeMedic-FINE, Explode.js)

characteristics.what_it_measures: Code size threshold where 90% of packages fall below
characteristics.evidence: Figure 2 showing CDF of token counts using gpt-5-mini tokenizer
characteristics.context: Indicates LLM-based approaches are well-suited for most npm packages

novelty: First systematic evaluation of LLM-only agent framework for taint-style vulnerability detection and confirmation
LLMVD.js achieves 84% confirmation rate vs <22% for prior program-analysis tools without static/dynamic analysis engines
Discovered 36 previously undocumented vulnerabilities in recently released npm packages
Novel multi-stage ReAct pipeline decomposing detection into Finder, Judge, Constraints Inferencer, and Exploiter stages
open_problems: Scaling to larger packages beyond 147K tokens
Reducing false negatives in complex call chains
Cost analysis and optimization for large-scale scanning
